article_title: CERN Discovers a Heavy Cousin of the Proton, Only the Second Baryon With Two Charm Quarks Ever Observed

content_preview.title: CERN Discovers a Heavy Cousin of the Proton, Only the Second Baryon With Two Charm Quarks Ever Observed
content_preview.summary: The LHCb experiment identifies the Xicc+ baryon at 7-sigma significance, bringing the total hadrons found at the LHC to 80.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The LHCb Collaboration at CERN's Large Hadron Collider has discovered a new subatomic particle composed of two charm quarks and one down quark, making it only the second baryon with two heavy quarks ever observed. The particle, designated Xicc+ (pronounced "ksai-cc-plus"), was presented on March 17, 2026, at the Moriond conference in La Thuile, Italy, and represents the first new particle identified since the LHCb detector completed its major upgrade in 2023, according to [CERN](htt...

editor_notes.content_quality: Excellent particle physics article with clear explanation.
editor_notes.source_verification: CERN official news confirmed: Xicc+, two charm quarks + down quark, 7-sigma, 4x proton mass, second doubly charmed baryon, 80th LHC hadron, upgraded detector, QCD significance.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims verified from primary CERN source.
